h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I think I mark B. `city value` could fit every row, so maybe that fails the value test, while the exact city name belongs to this one first-row cell. So `Value:` should be the printed city name there; is “fits every row” the reject sign?

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Three quarter-note beats fill the bar. Top number is three beats; bottom number says quarter note is the beat unit. Count is full, not faster.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to confirm the short-line diagram as the proof mark, completing the tutor's requested task straightforwardly.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: LABEL goes on the 3-to-tiles line and the 4-to-quarter-note-card line. BAR-PROOF goes on the bar line after the third quarter-note beat. The number-lines label count and beat unit; the bar line shows this bar is full.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 performs the new sorting task introduced by T3 — physically assigning the LABEL and BAR-PROOF tags to distinct marks — enacting a functional distinction between labeling and proof that was not operative in earlier turns.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly acknowledges the old route (short lines) cannot settle proof — the stock-taking contrast — and introduces a new public mechanism (covering the numbers, adding LABEL/BAR-PROOF tags, adopting the 'rehearsal reader' role) that requires the learner to do categorically different sorting work.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The cover-strip plus dual-tag sorting device is precisely fitted to L3's blockage — the learner could not distinguish labeling from proof — and gives an actionable, transferable criterion (what a rehearsal reader needs) rather than a renamed version of the diagram task, though it remains tutor-directed rather than fully learner-generated.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Under the measure, complete three short labels from the sorted marks: 3 = ___; 4 = ___; bar line after beat three = ___.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 builds on the sorted categories from L4 by asking the learner to complete fill-in labels, which is responsive to the completed sorting but does not substantially change the task frame or evidence standard — it consolidates rather than adapts to a revised learner framing.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The cover-strip plus dual-tag sorting device is precisely fitted to L3's blockage — the learner could not distinguish labeling from proof — and gives an actionable, transferable criterion (what a rehearsal reader needs) rather than a renamed version of the diagram task, though it remains tutor-directed rather than fully learner-generated.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to continue naming objects for each arrow in sequence, filling blanks with the same frame already established.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: With the head covered, the source is the table. Force of table on cart. The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 visibly applies the new arrowhead-covered test introduced by T3, naming the source object from contact marks rather than arrowhead direction, and places the card — a meaningfully different action from the prior arrow-direction-driven attempts.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name. New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, "force of ___ on cart." If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly marks the old route's failure ('the arrowhead is still taking over the name') and introduces a new physical device — covering the arrowhead with a strip — that forces the learner to work from contact marks rather than direction, satisfying both the stock-taking contrast and the new public mechanism requirements.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, "force of ___ on cart." If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is well-fitted: covering the arrowhead removes the specific distractor (direction cue) that was hijacking the learner's naming, gives the learner a concrete, repeatable criterion ('if the noun fits'), and is visibly different from the prior route of simply restating the frame.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.
